Following Florian Lipowitz’s retirement yesterday after a crash, while he was sitting in fifth place in the general classification, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe’s strategy has certainly been shaken up. If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. Now, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe will likely pin all its hopes on Evenepoel to hold onto that second-place spot in the general classification, which would mark his best-ever result in the Tour de France.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T11:50:00+00:00"},"updatedBy":null,"createdBy":"Aileen","status":"pending","createdAt":"2026-07-22T10:56:23+00:00"},"locale":"en","_id":"question-6a60a1d774054351de0dfedd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784721060901,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"updatedBy":"bvittek","slug":"3-2-5-leaders","id":1351013,"createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","title":"3 + 2 = 5 leaders","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","picto":"liv_breakaway","text":["Anthony Turgis (Total Energies) and then Ben Healy (EF Education- EsyPost) join the lead group.

talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"pinned":false,"locale":"en","stage":17,"type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"title":"Where are the top 10 riders on GC?","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:57:58+02:00","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:57:58+02:00","updatedBy":"bvittek","id":1351205,"slug":"where-are-the-top-10-riders-on-gc","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T14:57:58+02:00","picto":"liv_yellow_jersey","text":["According to Capgemini’s trackers, Remco Evenepoel (2nd), Paul Seixas (4th), Juan Ayuso (5th), and Mattias Skjelmose (6th) are in the front group with Tadej Pogacar.
But Isaac del Toro (3rd), Tom Pidcock (8th), Jordan Jegat (9th) and Yannis Voisard (10th) are in the second group, trailing by 25 seconds.
Lenny Martinez (7th) is no longer tracked after he swapped bikes due to a crash."],"_id":"d02d0f6e684c157283d09dba053c5169b03f5f7b6ed581975f0caf98d5d27144","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784725079125,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:where-are-the-top-10-riders-on-gc","model":"news"}]

The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”.
The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: Chazal, Casino and AG2r. Now with the leading retailer Decathlon and the shipping company CMA CGM as its title sponsors, it has featured 32 times in the Tour de France, where it has been a permanent fixture since 1997.
Olav Kooij’s success in Pau marked the team’s 23rd stage win in the Tour, the first since Felix Gall conquered the Col de la Loze in 2023. Paul Seixas is currently battling for the white jersey - conquered by Benoît Salmon (1999) and Pierre Latour (2018) in the team history - and a podium finish. But the team dreams much bigger with their French rising star: they want to win the Tour, and thus surpass the places of 2nd overall achieved by Jean-Christophe Péraud (2014) and Romain Bardet (2016)."],"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","_id":"8ea4941c0e9f8eaaec7ae7c6788bf7f4ced8eb7e521e306740d06447b0e9c045","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784717875598,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","model":"news"},{"slug":"welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-17-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","id":1350658,"updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:51:54+02:00","title":"Welcome to the live coverage of stage 17 of the Tour de France 2026","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:51:04+02:00","text":["After the time trial ruled by Remco Evenepoel, the peloton return to action with a 174.7 km stage from Chambéry to Voiron on rolling terrain. ","context":{"event":"tdf","stage":17,"locale":"en","category":null,"millesime":2026,"name":"tdf"},"_id":"6a60b3f2d7dc70a9f508ef96","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:13:38+00:00","createdBy":"attr42","status":"pending","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open Following Florian Lipowitz’s retirement yesterday after a crash, while he was sitting in fifth place in the general classification, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe’s strategy has certainly been shaken up. If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. Now, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe will likely pin all its hopes on Evenepoel to hold onto that second-place spot in the general classification, which would mark his best-ever result in the Tour de France.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T11:50:00+00:00"},"updatedBy":null,"createdBy":"Aileen","status":"pending","createdAt":"2026-07-22T10:56:23+00:00"},"locale":"en","_id":"question-6a60a1d774054351de0dfedd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784721060901,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"updatedBy":"bvittek","slug":"3-2-5-leaders","id":1351013,"createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","title":"3 + 2 = 5 leaders","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","picto":"liv_breakaway","text":["Anthony Turgis (Total Energies) and then Ben Healy (EF Education- EsyPost) join the lead group.

talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"id":1351205,"slug":"where-are-the-top-10-riders-on-gc","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T15:05:22+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:57:58+02:00","title":"Where are the top 10 riders on GC?","text":["According to Capgemini’s trackers, Remco Evenepoel (2nd), Paul Seixas (4th), Juan Ayuso (5th), and Mattias Skjelmose (6th) are in the front group with Tadej Pogacar.
But Isaac del Toro (3rd), Tom Pidcock (8th), Jordan Jegat (9th) and Yannis Voisard (10th) are in the second group, trailing by 25 seconds.
Lenny Martinez (7th) is no longer tracked after he swapped bikes due to a crash."],"picto":"liv_yellow_jersey","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T14:57:58+02:00","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","stage":17,"locale":"en","pinned":false,"year":2026,"highlight":true,"type":"liv","_id":"d02d0f6e684c157283d09dba053c5169b03f5f7b6ed581975f0caf98d5d27144","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784725522311,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:where-are-the-top-10-riders-on-gc","model":"news"}]

[{"updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","title":"A home start for Decathlon CMA CGM","slug":"a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","id":1350672,"updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","picto":"liv_story","text":["Born in 1992 under the guidance of Vincent Lavenu, Decathlon CMA CGM remains true to its Savoyard roots although it has undergone a metamorphosis over the last few years. The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”.
The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: Chazal, Casino and AG2r. Now with the leading retailer Decathlon and the shipping company CMA CGM as its title sponsors, it has featured 32 times in the Tour de France, where it has been a permanent fixture since 1997.
Olav Kooij’s success in Pau marked the team’s 23rd stage win in the Tour, the first since Felix Gall conquered the Col de la Loze in 2023. Paul Seixas is currently battling for the white jersey - conquered by Benoît Salmon (1999) and Pierre Latour (2018) in the team history - and a podium finish. But the team dreams much bigger with their French rising star: they want to win the Tour, and thus surpass the places of 2nd overall achieved by Jean-Christophe Péraud (2014) and Romain Bardet (2016)."],"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","_id":"8ea4941c0e9f8eaaec7ae7c6788bf7f4ced8eb7e521e306740d06447b0e9c045","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784717875598,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","model":"news"},{"slug":"welcome-to-the-live-coverage-of-stage-17-of-the-tour-de-france-2026","id":1350658,"updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:51:54+02:00","title":"Welcome to the live coverage of stage 17 of the Tour de France 2026","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:51:04+02:00","text":["After the time trial ruled by Remco Evenepoel, the peloton return to action with a 174.7 km stage from Chambéry to Voiron on rolling terrain. ","context":{"event":"tdf","stage":17,"locale":"en","category":null,"millesime":2026,"name":"tdf"},"_id":"6a60b3f2d7dc70a9f508ef96","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:13:38+00:00","createdBy":"attr42","status":"pending","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open Following Florian Lipowitz’s retirement yesterday after a crash, while he was sitting in fifth place in the general classification, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe’s strategy has certainly been shaken up. If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. Now, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe will likely pin all its hopes on Evenepoel to hold onto that second-place spot in the general classification, which would mark his best-ever result in the Tour de France.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T11:50:00+00:00"},"updatedBy":null,"createdBy":"Aileen","status":"pending","createdAt":"2026-07-22T10:56:23+00:00"},"locale":"en","_id":"question-6a60a1d774054351de0dfedd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784721060901,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"updatedBy":"bvittek","slug":"3-2-5-leaders","id":1351013,"createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","title":"3 + 2 = 5 leaders","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","picto":"liv_breakaway","text":["Anthony Turgis (Total Energies) and then Ben Healy (EF Education- EsyPost) join the lead group.

talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"id":1351205,"slug":"where-are-the-top-10-riders-on-gc","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T15:05:22+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:57:58+02:00","title":"Where are the top 10 riders on GC?","text":["According to Capgemini’s trackers, Remco Evenepoel (2nd), Paul Seixas (4th), Juan Ayuso (5th), and Mattias Skjelmose (6th) are in the front group with Tadej Pogacar.

","context":{"event":"tdf","stage":17,"locale":"en","category":null,"millesime":2026,"name":"tdf"},"_id":"6a60b3f2d7dc70a9f508ef96","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:13:38+00:00","createdBy":"attr42","status":"pending","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

of this kind ? ","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","title":"A home start for Decathlon CMA CGM","slug":"a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","id":1350672,"updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","picto":"liv_story","text":["Born in 1992 under the guidance of Vincent Lavenu, Decathlon CMA CGM remains true to its Savoyard roots although it has undergone a metamorphosis over the last few years. The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”.
The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: Chazal, Casino and AG2r. Now with the leading retailer Decathlon and the shipping company CMA CGM as its title sponsors, it has featured 32 times in the Tour de France, where it has been a permanent fixture since 1997.
Olav Kooij’s success in Pau marked the team’s 23rd stage win in the Tour, the first since Felix Gall conquered the Col de la Loze in 2023. Paul Seixas is currently battling for the white jersey - conquered by Benoît Salmon (1999) and Pierre Latour (2018) in the team history - and a podium finish. But the team dreams much bigger with their French rising star: they want to win the Tour, and thus surpass the places of 2nd overall achieved by Jean-Christophe Péraud (2014) and Romain Bardet (2016)."],"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","_id":"8ea4941c0e9f8eaaec7ae7c6788bf7f4ced8eb7e521e306740d06447b0e9c045","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784717875598,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","model":"news"},{"id":1351205,"slug":"where-are-the-top-10-riders-on-gc","updatedBy":"bvittek","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T15:05:22+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:57:58+02:00","title":"Where are the top 10 riders on GC?","text":["According to Capgemini’s trackers, Remco Evenepoel (2nd), Paul Seixas (4th), Juan Ayuso (5th), and Mattias Skjelmose (6th) are in the front group with Tadej Pogacar.
But Isaac del Toro (3rd), Tom Pidcock (8th), Jordan Jegat (9th) and Yannis Voisard (10th) are in the second group, trailing by 25 seconds.
Lenny Martinez (7th) is no longer tracked after he swapped bikes due to a crash."],"picto":"liv_yellow_jersey","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T14:57:58+02:00","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","stage":17,"locale":"en","pinned":false,"year":2026,"highlight":true,"type":"liv","_id":"d02d0f6e684c157283d09dba053c5169b03f5f7b6ed581975f0caf98d5d27144","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784725522311,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:where-are-the-top-10-riders-on-gc","model":"news"}]

That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open Following Florian Lipowitz’s retirement yesterday after a crash, while he was sitting in fifth place in the general classification, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe’s strategy has certainly been shaken up. If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

of this kind ? ","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","title":"A home start for Decathlon CMA CGM","slug":"a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","id":1350672,"updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","picto":"liv_story","text":["Born in 1992 under the guidance of Vincent Lavenu, Decathlon CMA CGM remains true to its Savoyard roots although it has undergone a metamorphosis over the last few years. The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”.
The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: Chazal, Casino and AG2r. Now with the leading retailer Decathlon and the shipping company CMA CGM as its title sponsors, it has featured 32 times in the Tour de France, where it has been a permanent fixture since 1997.
Olav Kooij’s success in Pau marked the team’s 23rd stage win in the Tour, the first since Felix Gall conquered the Col de la Loze in 2023. Paul Seixas is currently battling for the white jersey - conquered by Benoît Salmon (1999) and Pierre Latour (2018) in the team history - and a podium finish. ","context":{"event":"tdf","stage":17,"locale":"en","category":null,"millesime":2026,"name":"tdf"},"_id":"6a60b3f2d7dc70a9f508ef96","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:13:38+00:00","createdBy":"attr42","status":"pending","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open Following Florian Lipowitz’s retirement yesterday after a crash, while he was sitting in fifth place in the general classification, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe’s strategy has certainly been shaken up. If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. Now, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe will likely pin all its hopes on Evenepoel to hold onto that second-place spot in the general classification, which would mark his best-ever result in the Tour de France.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T11:50:00+00:00"},"updatedBy":null,"createdBy":"Aileen","status":"pending","createdAt":"2026-07-22T10:56:23+00:00"},"locale":"en","_id":"question-6a60a1d774054351de0dfedd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784721060901,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"updatedBy":"bvittek","slug":"3-2-5-leaders","id":1351013,"createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","title":"3 + 2 = 5 leaders","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","picto":"liv_breakaway","text":["Anthony Turgis (Total Energies) and then Ben Healy (EF Education- EsyPost) join the lead group.

talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","title":"A home start for Decathlon CMA CGM","slug":"a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","id":1350672,"updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","picto":"liv_story","text":["Born in 1992 under the guidance of Vincent Lavenu, Decathlon CMA CGM remains true to its Savoyard roots although it has undergone a metamorphosis over the last few years. The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”.
The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: Chazal, Casino and AG2r. Now with the leading retailer Decathlon and the shipping company CMA CGM as its title sponsors, it has featured 32 times in the Tour de France, where it has been a permanent fixture since 1997.
Olav Kooij’s success in Pau marked the team’s 23rd stage win in the Tour, the first since Felix Gall conquered the Col de la Loze in 2023. Paul Seixas is currently battling for the white jersey - conquered by Benoît Salmon (1999) and Pierre Latour (2018) in the team history - and a podium finish. But the team dreams much bigger with their French rising star: they want to win the Tour, and thus surpass the places of 2nd overall achieved by Jean-Christophe Péraud (2014) and Romain Bardet (2016)."],"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","_id":"8ea4941c0e9f8eaaec7ae7c6788bf7f4ced8eb7e521e306740d06447b0e9c045","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784717875598,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","model":"news"},{"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","stage":17,"locale":"en","pinned":false,"text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) led the charge with Ben Healy (EF Education-EasyPost). That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open Following Florian Lipowitz’s retirement yesterday after a crash, while he was sitting in fifth place in the general classification, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe’s strategy has certainly been shaken up. If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

of this kind ? ","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","title":"A home start for Decathlon CMA CGM","slug":"a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","id":1350672,"updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","picto":"liv_story","text":["Born in 1992 under the guidance of Vincent Lavenu, Decathlon CMA CGM remains true to its Savoyard roots although it has undergone a metamorphosis over the last few years. The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”.
The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: Chazal, Casino and AG2r. Now with the leading retailer Decathlon and the shipping company CMA CGM as its title sponsors, it has featured 32 times in the Tour de France, where it has been a permanent fixture since 1997.
Olav Kooij’s success in Pau marked the team’s 23rd stage win in the Tour, the first since Felix Gall conquered the Col de la Loze in 2023. Paul Seixas is currently battling for the white jersey - conquered by Benoît Salmon (1999) and Pierre Latour (2018) in the team history - and a podium finish. But the team dreams much bigger with their French rising star: they want to win the Tour, and thus surpass the places of 2nd overall achieved by Jean-Christophe Péraud (2014) and Romain Bardet (2016)."],"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","_id":"8ea4941c0e9f8eaaec7ae7c6788bf7f4ced8eb7e521e306740d06447b0e9c045","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784717875598,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","model":"news"},{"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","stage":17,"locale":"en","pinned":false,"text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) led the charge with Ben Healy (EF Education-EasyPost). ","context":{"event":"tdf","stage":17,"locale":"en","category":null,"millesime":2026,"name":"tdf"},"_id":"6a60b3f2d7dc70a9f508ef96","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:13:38+00:00","createdBy":"attr42","status":"pending","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

of this kind ? ","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","title":"A home start for Decathlon CMA CGM","slug":"a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","id":1350672,"updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","picto":"liv_story","text":["Born in 1992 under the guidance of Vincent Lavenu, Decathlon CMA CGM remains true to its Savoyard roots although it has undergone a metamorphosis over the last few years. The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”.
The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: Chazal, Casino and AG2r. Now with the leading retailer Decathlon and the shipping company CMA CGM as its title sponsors, it has featured 32 times in the Tour de France, where it has been a permanent fixture since 1997.
Olav Kooij’s success in Pau marked the team’s 23rd stage win in the Tour, the first since Felix Gall conquered the Col de la Loze in 2023. Paul Seixas is currently battling for the white jersey - conquered by Benoît Salmon (1999) and Pierre Latour (2018) in the team history - and a podium finish. But the team dreams much bigger with their French rising star: they want to win the Tour, and thus surpass the places of 2nd overall achieved by Jean-Christophe Péraud (2014) and Romain Bardet (2016)."],"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","_id":"8ea4941c0e9f8eaaec7ae7c6788bf7f4ced8eb7e521e306740d06447b0e9c045","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784717875598,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","model":"news"},{"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","stage":17,"locale":"en","pinned":false,"text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) led the charge with Ben Healy (EF Education-EasyPost). Julian Alaphilippe (Tudor) and Kévin Vauquelin (Netcompany Ineos) are still up there.

That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open Following Florian Lipowitz’s retirement yesterday after a crash, while he was sitting in fifth place in the general classification, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe’s strategy has certainly been shaken up. If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","title":"A home start for Decathlon CMA CGM","slug":"a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","id":1350672,"updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","picto":"liv_story","text":["Born in 1992 under the guidance of Vincent Lavenu, Decathlon CMA CGM remains true to its Savoyard roots although it has undergone a metamorphosis over the last few years. The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”.
The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: Chazal, Casino and AG2r. Now with the leading retailer Decathlon and the shipping company CMA CGM as its title sponsors, it has featured 32 times in the Tour de France, where it has been a permanent fixture since 1997.
Olav Kooij’s success in Pau marked the team’s 23rd stage win in the Tour, the first since Felix Gall conquered the Col de la Loze in 2023. Paul Seixas is currently battling for the white jersey - conquered by Benoît Salmon (1999) and Pierre Latour (2018) in the team history - and a podium finish. But the team dreams much bigger with their French rising star: they want to win the Tour, and thus surpass the places of 2nd overall achieved by Jean-Christophe Péraud (2014) and Romain Bardet (2016)."],"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","_id":"8ea4941c0e9f8eaaec7ae7c6788bf7f4ced8eb7e521e306740d06447b0e9c045","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784717875598,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","model":"news"},{"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","stage":17,"locale":"en","pinned":false,"text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) led the charge with Ben Healy (EF Education-EasyPost). That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
Since 2016, there is also a U19 team, from which emerged Paul Seixas (Decathlon CMA CGM), Romain Grégoire (Groupama-FDJ United), and Oscar Onley."],"picto":"liv_story","createdBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","year":2026,"highlight":false,"_id":"6d0fa1fff41d712ef7c4ed5583754d63513877e82f9b82b013b50281e51e6bd3","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784718432842,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:homegrown-talents-paret-peintre-veistroffer-jorgenson","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","title":"Already the third climb of the day","createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:40:17+02:00","id":1351109,"slug":"already-the-third-climb-of-the-day","updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) drives the break at the bottom of the 3.5 km ascent (average gradient: 6.8%).

of this kind ? ","event":{"trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","name":"Le Tour de France","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:23+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello Paul ! We are indeed seeing numerous attacks aimed at forming a breakaway in these opening kilometres of the race. This is due to the unique profile of this 17th stage. With a hard start leading up to the Col des Prés, followed by a long descent and then around a hundred kilometres of mostly flat terrain, the stage is, on paper, difficult for the teams at the front of the peloton to control. In other words, a breakaway stands a strong chance to win. However, since so many riders want to get into the lead group, the attacks are coming thick and fast without success, as each acceleration cancels out the last. It could still take several kilometres for the right move to finally break clear of the peloton.","updatedAt":null},"_id":"6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","context":{"stage":17,"event":"tdf","locale":"en","millesime":2026,"category":null,"name":"tdf"},"updatedBy":null},"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:52:00+00:00","locale":"en","widget":"involve","_id":"question-6a60bcfa9ff5248a3d0889bd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784724743419,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"updatedAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","title":"A home start for Decathlon CMA CGM","slug":"a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","id":1350672,"updatedBy":"bvittek","stageType":"PLN","createdBy":"bvittek","picto":"liv_story","text":["Born in 1992 under the guidance of Vincent Lavenu, Decathlon CMA CGM remains true to its Savoyard roots although it has undergone a metamorphosis over the last few years. The team was established in Chambéry and has been based there ever since, but it now describes itself as “international and based in the French Alps”.
The oldest French squad in the peloton has had three previous main sponsors: Chazal, Casino and AG2r. Now with the leading retailer Decathlon and the shipping company CMA CGM as its title sponsors, it has featured 32 times in the Tour de France, where it has been a permanent fixture since 1997.
Olav Kooij’s success in Pau marked the team’s 23rd stage win in the Tour, the first since Felix Gall conquered the Col de la Loze in 2023. Paul Seixas is currently battling for the white jersey - conquered by Benoît Salmon (1999) and Pierre Latour (2018) in the team history - and a podium finish. But the team dreams much bigger with their French rising star: they want to win the Tour, and thus surpass the places of 2nd overall achieved by Jean-Christophe Péraud (2014) and Romain Bardet (2016)."],"publicationAt":"2026-07-22T12:57:55+02:00","locale":"en","pinned":false,"stage":17,"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","_id":"8ea4941c0e9f8eaaec7ae7c6788bf7f4ced8eb7e521e306740d06447b0e9c045","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"news-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784717875598,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:a-home-start-for-decathlon-cma-cgm","model":"news"},{"highlight":false,"year":2026,"type":"liv","stage":17,"locale":"en","pinned":false,"text":["Felix Engelhardt (Jayco AlUla) led the charge with Ben Healy (EF Education-EasyPost). ","context":{"event":"tdf","stage":17,"locale":"en","category":null,"millesime":2026,"name":"tdf"},"_id":"6a60b3f2d7dc70a9f508ef96","createdAt":"2026-07-22T12:13:38+00:00","createdBy":"attr42","status":"pending","response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! That is indeed the key question regarding this 17th stage which features a unique profile ! With the opening tens of kilometres proving grueling in the Bauges massif, including the climb of the Col des Prés (3.5 km at 6.8 %), the start of the stage is ideal for the formation of a fairly large breakaway. Given the relatively manageable gradients, many riders could aim to join the move and potentially open up a gap on the peloton. However, with over 100 kilometres remaining to the finish, alternating between flat sections and false flats that are more suited to sprinters, we could see a battle unfold between the leaders and certain sprinter teams, for whom this might be their last chance of the race. The outcome and eventual winner of this stage are therefore wide open Following Florian Lipowitz’s retirement yesterday after a crash, while he was sitting in fifth place in the general classification, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe’s strategy has certainly been shaken up. If the German rider had remained alongside Remco Evenepoel (second overall), his team could have employed aggressive tactics to target mountain stage wins or even attempt to get Lipowitz onto the podium. Now, Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe will likely pin all its hopes on Evenepoel to hold onto that second-place spot in the general classification, which would mark his best-ever result in the Tour de France.","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T11:50:00+00:00"},"updatedBy":null,"createdBy":"Aileen","status":"pending","createdAt":"2026-07-22T10:56:23+00:00"},"locale":"en","_id":"question-6a60a1d774054351de0dfedd","_bind":"publication_en-2026-17","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-17","_updatedAt":1784721060901,"_parent":"stage-2026:0cd1639487e7086115459614e40423e7b928c28644ba27c13a841786d67f6106","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"stage":17,"pinned":false,"locale":"en","type":"liv","highlight":true,"year":2026,"updatedBy":"bvittek","slug":"3-2-5-leaders","id":1351013,"createdAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","title":"3 + 2 = 5 leaders","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T14:21:03+02:00","picto":"liv_breakaway","text":["Anthony Turgis (Total Energies) and then Ben Healy (EF Education- EsyPost) join the lead group.

talent: Paret-Peintre, Veistroffer, Jorgenson…","updatedAt":"2026-07-22T13:07:12+02:00","publicationAt":"2026-07-22T13:06:46+02:00","text":["Today’s start in Chambéry is the perfect occasion to celebrate the long history of riders development in the area. Chambéry Cyclisme Formation is a cycling club born in 2001 that operated as a feeder for AG2R La Mondiale, before taking the name AG2R Citroën U23 in 2021, Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale Development in 2024, and now Decathlon CMA CGM Development Team.
The team has supported the rise of many elite talents such as Romain Bardet, who embodied the excellence of the local setup for years. The peloton of the Tour de France 2026 features eight riders who have come through the team and became key riders for Decathlon CMA CGM or other squads: Matteo Jorgenson (Visma-Lease a Bike), Nico Denz (Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe), Silvan Dillier (Alpecin-Premier Tech), Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal Quick-Step), Baptiste Veistroffer (Lotto Intermarché), Alex Baudin (EF Education-EasyPost), Sebastian Berwick (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA), and Aurélien Paret-Peintre (Decathlon CMA CGM).
